title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: It's the day of the party and I think everyone's going to show up in a few hours. I deep cleaned yesterday, but I still need to pick up the house a little bit before all the guests arrive. I'm going to start by putting the dishes away.
Finally, I'm going to vacuum.
>> You're going to be the DD.

title: will / shall
examples: It's really delicious, but I'll resist the temptation.
This costs the same as one pack of those strawberries. We're getting this. We'll send any extras with your family.
I'll do the recycling as well.

Olives, crackers, dessert plate. M. Pro tip, buy packaged food and put it on a plate. That way it looks like you made it. Tip: Never throw away your boxes because you can use them again for leftover food after the party. I've made that mistake many times. The last thing I have to do is heat up the chicken. I will just use the air fryer for that so it's not a hassle.
>> Time for DJ Callie. What kind of music does your dad like? He plays the saxophone, so I think he'll like jazz music. Let's do that.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: wall. This is a huge cart. Do you think we can fill it? I think we can.
>> Really good. You can't pass by the free samples and not grab one. It's a rule.
Can't go wrong with that.
I think everybody likes takangjang. And it says you can heat it in the air fryer and it's even more delicious. So, we got to get this. 2 4 6 18. That's more than enough, I think.
I bet you this is filled with air. Can I check?

title: have to / must
examples: I had to check it and make sure it's good. Quality assurance test.

Olives, crackers, dessert plate. M. Pro tip, buy packaged food and put it on a plate. That way it looks like you made it. Tip: Never throw away your boxes because you can use them again for leftover food after the party. I've made that mistake many times. The last thing I have to do is heat up the chicken. I will just use the air fryer for that so it's not a hassle.
Wow, you didn't have to bring all this.
>> Designated driver. So, he cannot drink alcohol tonight. He has to drive home.

title: should (advice)
examples: Which one should we buy? These look pretty good, actually.

title: had better / it's time
examples: Now, it's time to set out the food. I usually like to cook everything myself and prepare a big spread, but unfortunately I was too busy to do that this time. So, I ordered some food and grabbed some simple appetizers and finger food from the grocery store.

title: Can/Could/Would you...?
examples: I bet you this is filled with air. Can I check?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: Let's see if they have some fancy cheeses in the deli. Something like these maybe with crackers. Salami cheese and crackers. It's a classic combo.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: I bet you this is filled with air. Can I check?

title: have something done
examples: Oh, they have the fried chicken ice cream.
